last_week_digest.title: Payload Ransomware claims the hack of Royal Bahrain Hospital
last_week_digest.tldr: Royal Bahrain Hospital was breached by Payload Ransomware, with 110 GB of data stolen, affecting patients from Bahrain and neighbouring countries, and threatening to release the data if a ransom is not paid by March 23, highlighting the vulnerability of healthcare facilities to cyber attacks.

last_week_digest.title: Attackers are exploiting AI faster than defenders can keep up, new report warns
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers are exploiting AI faster than defenders can respond, with cybercriminals and state-sponsored groups using AI to identify weaknesses and establish persistence at machine speed, leaving defenders struggling to keep up with slower, human-oriented processes, and forcing organisations to reevaluate their risk tolerance and consider automated remediation.

last_week_digest.title: Starbucks data breach impacts 889 employees
last_week_digest.tldr: Starbucks reported a data breach affecting 889 employees after phishing attacks targeted its employee portal, potentially exposing personal information, including names, Social Security numbers, and bank account details, between January 19 and February 11, with the company offering 24 months of identity protection to affected individuals.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware incident responder gave info to BlackCat cybercriminals during negotiations, DOJ alleges
last_week_digest.tldr: Angelo Martino, a ransomware incident responder, allegedly gave information to BlackCat cybercriminals, earning $1.2 million, and faces up to 20 years in prison, signalling a significant breach of trust in the industry, with far-reaching consequences for businesses and cybersecurity firms, particularly DigitalMint, where Martino was employed, and highlighting the need for increased transparency and oversight in ransom negotiations.

last_week_digest.title: In Other News: N8n Flaw Exploited, Slopoly Malware, Interpol Cybercrime Crackdown
last_week_digest.tldr: A Telus Digital data breach occurred, while Linux AppArmor vulnerabilities allowed root privileges, and a US defense contractor was linked to Coruna exploits, affecting various global entities, with potential significant financial losses and operational disruptions for the companies involved, highlighting ongoing cybersecurity threats.

last_week_digest.title: Russia-linked APT uses DRILLAPP backdoor to spy on Ukrainian targets
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia-linked APT targeted Ukrainian entities with the DRILLAPP backdoor, using Microsoft Edge debugging for stealth, affecting various organizations and indicating ongoing espionage efforts with around 2 variants detected in February 2026, allowing attackers to access files, microphone, and camera, with potential significant business consequences.

last_week_digest.title: Russia-linked espionage campaign targeting Ukraine using Starlink and charity lures
last_week_digest.tldr: A Russia-linked hacker group targeted Ukrainian organizations with spyware disguised in documents about Starlink and a charity, affecting an unknown number of users, with potential significant operational consequences, signalling an escalation in cyber-espionage efforts by nation-state actors in the region, with Laundry Bear's tactics posing a threat to Ukraine's defence and institutions.

last_week_digest.title: CISA flags Wing FTP Server flaw as actively exploited in attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Over 10,000 Wing FTP Server customers worldwide are at risk due to an actively exploited vulnerability, with the US Air Force, Sony, and Airbus among those affected, potentially leading to remote code execution attacks and significant business disruption.

last_week_digest.title: China-Nexus Hackers Skulk in Southeast Asian Military Orgs for Years
last_week_digest.tldr: Southeast Asian military organisations were breached, with hackers maintaining access for years, using novel backdoors and evasion techniques, impacting regional security and potentially compromising sensitive information, with long-term consequences for national defence and global stability, affecting undisclosed numbers of personnel and systems.

last_week_digest.title: Security Firm Executive Targeted in Sophisticated Phishing Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: A security firm executive was targeted in a sophisticated phishing attack, utilising DKIM-signed emails and trusted redirect infrastructure, highlighting the evolving threat landscape and potential business consequences for high-profile individuals, with the attack's complexity signalling a growing trend in targeted cybercrime, affecting global organisations.

last_week_digest.title: CL-STA-1087 targets military capabilities since 2020
last_week_digest.tldr: China-linked APT group CL-STA-1087 has targeted Southeast Asian militaries since 2020, using AppleChris and MemFun malware to collect sensitive files on military operations and organizational structures, with attackers demonstrating strategic operational patience and a focus on highly targeted intelligence collection, impacting regional security and military capabilities.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
last_week_digest.tldr: A 7-stage phishing attack targeted cybersecurity firm Outpost24, attempting to trick a C-suite executive into divulging credentials, leveraging trusted brands and domains, but ultimately proved unsuccessful, highlighting the evolving sophistication of cyber threats and the need for robust security measures to protect business leaders and sensitive information.

last_week_digest.title: Iran's cyberattack against med tech firm is 'just the beginning'
last_week_digest.tldr: Iran's cyberattack on med tech firm Stryker, causing a global network outage, signals more aggressive cyber-ops as the war escalates, with businesses at risk, particularly those in the global economy and healthcare supply chain, as Iran targets civilian assets, seeking to disrupt and exploit vulnerabilities, with 1 major US corporation already affected.

last_week_digest.title: UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms
last_week_digest.tldr: UK Companies House exposed details of millions of firms due to a vulnerability, potentially allowing alteration of records, affecting millions of companies, with significant business consequences and reputational damage, highlighting the need for robust cybersecurity measures to protect sensitive data.

last_week_digest.title: GlassWorm malware hits 400+ code repos on GitHub, npm, VSCode, OpenVSX
last_week_digest.tldr: Over 400 code repositories on GitHub, npm, and VSCode/OpenVSX were hit by GlassWorm malware, targeting cryptocurrency wallet data and developer credentials, with 433 compromised components identified, affecting 200 Python and 151 JS/TS repositories, and 72 extensions, signalling a significant supply-chain attack with far-reaching business consequences.

last_week_digest.title: Russia-linked hackers use advanced iPhone exploit to target Ukrainians
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia-linked hackers used the DarkSword iPhone exploit to target Ukrainians, stealing sensitive data with little user interaction, affecting visitors to compromised websites, including a news outlet and court website, with potential financial motives, targeting cryptocurrency platforms like Coinbase and Binance.

last_week_digest.title: EDR killers are now standard equipment in ransomware attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Ransomware attackers use EDR killers to disable security software, with nearly 90 tools tracked by ESET Research, allowing brief windows for encryption without detection, affecting businesses globally with significant operational consequences and financial losses, signaling a need for proactive monitoring and adaptive defensive strategies to counter evolving threats.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware gang exploits Cisco flaw in zero-day attacks since January
last_week_digest.tldr: The Interlock ransomware gang exploited a Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center flaw in zero-day attacks since January, compromising enterprise firewalls, with Cisco patching the issue on March 4, warning of remote code execution risks, and urging customers to upgrade, after the gang had a 36-day head start to compromise organizations.

last_week_digest.title: Interlock group exploiting the CISCO FMC flaw CVE-2026-20131 36 days before disclosure
last_week_digest.tldr: The Interlock ransomware group exploited a Cisco FMC zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2026-20131, 36 days before disclosure, affecting Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center and Security Cloud Control, with a CVSS score of 10.0, allowing remote code execution, and potentially compromising multiple organizations, including targets in various sectors, with Amazon researchers uncovering the activity.

last_week_digest.title: Cisco Firewall Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day in Interlock Ransomware Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: A Cisco firewall vulnerability was exploited as a zero-day in Interlock ransomware attacks, with evidence of exploitation since late January, and links to Russia, affecting an unspecified number of users, resulting in significant business disruption and potential financial losses, signalling a growing threat to global businesses from sophisticated cyber attacks.

last_week_digest.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra flaw in Ukrainian govt attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Russian hackers exploited a Zimbra Collaboration Suite flaw, affecting Ukrainian government entities, with potential global implications for hundreds of millions of users, including government agencies and businesses, highlighting the vulnerability of widely-used email software to state-backed cyber attacks.

last_week_digest.title: DarkSword emerges as powerful iOS exploit tool in global attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: DarkSword, an iOS exploit kit, has been used globally to steal data from Apple devices in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine, targeting iOS 18.4-18.7, with six vulnerabilities, including three zero-days, enabling full device compromise, and allowing attackers to extract sensitive data, including credentials and crypto wallet information, in a "hit-and-run" approach.

last_week_digest.title: Russian APT targets Ukraine via Zimbra XSS flaw CVE-2025-66376
last_week_digest.tldr: A Russian APT group exploited a Zimbra XSS flaw, CVE-2025-66376, to target Ukrainian users, stealing credentials and emails, with potential to compromise entire environments, affecting government entities and critical infrastructure, signalling increased cyber warfare risks for global businesses and organisations relying on Zimbra Collaboration.

last_week_digest.title: Security Firm Aura Discloses Data Breach Impacting 900,000 Records
last_week_digest.tldr: Security firm Aura suffered a data breach affecting 900,000 records after an employee fell victim to a targeted phone phishing attack, compromising a marketing tool, with significant business consequences likely to follow, impacting trust and operations globally.

last_week_digest.title: Interlock ransomware gang exploited Cisco firewall zero-day weeks before disclosure: Amazon
last_week_digest.tldr: Interlock ransomware gang exploited a Cisco firewall zero-day vulnerability, affecting numerous organisations, including the city of St. Paul, with attacks starting on January 26, weeks before public disclosure on March 4, causing significant operational downtime and data exposure, particularly in the education sector, with over 40% of their activity targeting schools.

last_week_digest.title: Iran-linked actors use Telegram as C2 in malware attacks on dissidents
last_week_digest.tldr: Iran-linked actors targeted dissidents and journalists worldwide, using Telegram as a command-and-control infrastructure to deliver malware, enabling surveillance and data theft, with the FBI warning of ongoing cyber campaigns by Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security, reflecting rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, affecting Windows systems since late 2023.

last_week_digest.title: Russian hackers go after high-value targets through Signal
last_week_digest.tldr: Russian hackers are targeting high-value individuals through Signal, compromising thousands of accounts via phishing, exploiting the "linked devices" feature, with potential financial and data losses, signalling a significant threat to global business and government communications security.

last_week_digest.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
last_week_digest.tldr: The Beast Gang's ransomware server exposure reveals a systematic attack on network backups, a key tactic, with potentially significant business consequences for affected organisations, likely numbering in the hundreds, given the group's aggressive approach, underscoring the importance of robust backup security for global companies to mitigate such threats and associated financial losses.

last_week_digest.title: California city reports ransomware attack as LA transit agency finds ‘unauthorized activity’
last_week_digest.tldr: Foster City, California, with 34,000 residents, reported a ransomware attack, pausing public services and declaring a state of emergency, while the LA transit agency found unauthorized activity, limiting employee access to internal systems, causing technical issues for customers, but no data breach was found, affecting daily operations for thousands of commuters.

last_week_digest.title: US Confirms Handala Link to Iran Government Amid Takedown of Hackers’ Sites
last_week_digest.tldr: The US seized several domains used by Handala, a group linked to the Iran government, disrupting their cyber-enabled psychological operations, impacting their ability to conduct online activities, with the full extent of the disruption still unfolding, affecting their operational capacity.

last_week_digest.title: Pro-Ukraine hacker group Bearlyfy targets Russian companies with custom ransomware
last_week_digest.tldr: Pro-Ukraine hacker group Bearlyfy has targeted over 70 Russian companies with custom ransomware, demanding hundreds of thousands of dollars, with roughly one in five victims paying the ransom, causing significant financial and operational damage to large Russian businesses over the past year.

last_week_digest.title: China-linked Red Menshen APT deploys stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecom networks
last_week_digest.tldr: China-linked Red Menshen APT deployed stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecom networks, mainly in the Middle East and Asia, to spy on government targets, compromising critical infrastructure and threatening entire populations, with the group active since at least 2021 and using highly stealthy implants to maintain hidden access.

last_week_digest.title: Dutch Police discloses security breach after phishing attack
last_week_digest.tldr: The Dutch National Police disclosed a security breach following a phishing attack, with limited impact and no citizen data exposed. The incident is under investigation, with access to compromised systems blocked. This breach signals the need for stronger security measures, following a similar 2024 incident where police officers' work-related contact information was stolen, affecting multiple officers.

last_week_digest.title: CISA: New Langflow flaw actively exploited to hijack AI workflows
last_week_digest.tldr: Hackers are exploiting a critical Langflow flaw, CVE-2026-33017, to hijack AI workflows, with 145,000 GitHub users potentially affected, allowing remote code execution and data harvesting, prompting CISA to issue a warning and recommend security updates by April 8 to prevent further exploitation.

last_week_digest.title: Hightower Holding Data Breach Impacts 130,000
last_week_digest.tldr: Hightower Holding suffered a data breach, impacting 130,000 individuals, with hackers stealing names, Social Security numbers, and driver's license numbers, resulting in significant personal data exposure and potential financial losses, signalling a major concern for the industry regarding data protection and cybersecurity measures.
